##NAMES=Khinite
##RRUFFID=R080124
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Cu^2+^_3_PbTe^6+^O_6_(OH)_2_
##LOCALITY=400 ft. level, Empire mine, Tombstone, Arizona
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=William W. Pinch
##DESCRIPTION=Aggregates of dark emerald-green hexagonal prismatic crystals, this is the -3T polytype, previously known as parakhinite. Sample was used in the Hawthorne et al (2008) study
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral has been confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R080124
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 5.758 b: 5.758 c: 17.961 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 120 volume: 515.66 crystal system: hexagonal
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
